raw_consumer_statement: On XXXX, XXXX, I mistakenly sent a payment in the amount of {$580.00} to American Express ; I hit the wrong vendor on my online banking bill pay at my Bank. I immediately attempted to stop payment, but payment was via XXXX and could not be reversed, even though the system sends physical checks to all other vendors. I reached out to AmEx customer service and explained, and they said I would be issued a refund check in 10 days. I then received a letter from American Express errantly describing the transaction as a fraudulent charge and request for a charge reversal of {$580.00}. It was neither a fraudulent charge reversal nor {$580.00}. It was an errant payment in the amount of {$580.00}. American Express allocated my payment internally as a credit on an account. There is no account, as it was closed in XXXX and I can not get them to refund my payment they are wrongfully converted to their own use.
